text.title: Vision Application Patterns
text.summary: DocumentAnalyzer, ImageClassifier, ContentModerator, and VisionService production classes for vision LLM integration.

section.title: Document Analysis
section.p: DocumentAnalyzer wraps GPT-4o Vision to extract text and structured fields from documents including receipts, forms, and passports.

example.note: Always use json_mode=True for structured extraction and wrap json.loads in try/except.

section.title: Image Classification
section.p: ImageClassifier sends predefined categories with the image and receives a category, confidence score, and reasoning.

section.title: Content Moderation
section.p: ContentModerator checks images for policy violations and returns a structured severity flag. Never use as the sole moderation layer — false negative rate on subtly harmful content is non-zero.

example.code: # Moderation prompt pattern prompt = """Analyze for policy violations. Categories: violence, adult_content, hate_symbols, self_harm, illegal_activity. Return JSON: {"is_safe": bool, "flags": [str], "severity": "none|low|medium|high", "confidence": 0-1, "needs_human_review": bool} Set needs_human_review: true when confidence < 0.7."""
example.note: Normalize severity values (enforce lowercase) — "HIGH" vs "high" breaks downstream routing silently.

section.title: Production Vision Service
section.reference: VisionService with VisionConfig, retry, size validation, and media type detection
section.p: VisionService wraps all vision calls with size validation (reject over 20MB), base64 encoding, retry logic, and structured output parsing.

example.code: @dataclass class VisionConfig: model: str = "gpt-4o" max_image_size_mb: int = 20 detail: str = "auto" # "low" | "high" | "auto" max_retries: int = 3 timeout: int = 60

antipattern.description: Passing remote image URLs from user input directly to the API without validation.
antipattern.reason: Remote URLs embedded in prompts are fetched by the API server; URLs behind auth or expired pre-signed URLs cause silent failures.
